rag_rules: Fix unresolved property references by ensuring property definitions and constants are visible in the current scope or remove invalid clauses.
Resolve type errors in AGREE expressions by ensuring all identifiers refer to valid AADL features or declared helpers.
Address 'does not point to anything' diagnostics by validating property associations and terms reference valid AADL elements.
generic_rules: When repairing AGREE contracts, ensure all referenced identifiers are visible and properly typed in the current scope.
Remove or correct invalid AGREE clauses that reference undefined or unreachable elements.
Validate that property associations and terms in AADL models point to valid definitions or constants.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE expressions must have valid types; avoid '<error>' types by resolving root references.
Property references in AGREE must resolve to valid definitions or constants in the current scope.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE contracts to prevent type and resolution errors.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to fix unresolved references; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Avoid introducing invalid property associations or terms that do not resolve to valid AADL constructs.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about unreachable or non-existent property associations
AGREE validator diagnostics about property definition resolution
